anchorregistry
  • »
  • Watermarking
  • View page source

Watermarking

Generate SPDX-Anchor and DAPX-Anchor tags for registered artifacts.

  • ``SPDX-Anchor`` — for CODE artifacts. Extends the SPDX standard with an on-chain anchor.

  • ``DAPX-Anchor`` — for all other artifact types.

These examples run against Sepolia testnet (network="sepolia"). To run against Base mainnet: replace configure(network="sepolia") with configure(network="base"). All function calls, record structures, and output shapes are identical across networks.

[ ]:

# Cell 1 — setup
from anchorregistry import configure, watermark

configure(network="sepolia")

[ ]:

# Cell 2 — CODE type → SPDX-Anchor
# Pass artifact_type explicitly to skip an on-chain lookup
line = watermark("AR-2026-x1llnO1", artifact_type="CODE")
print(line)
# → SPDX-Anchor: anchorregistry.ai/AR-2026-x1llnO1

[ ]:

# Cell 3 — auto-resolve type from chain
# Omit artifact_type — the function fetches the type from the contract
line = watermark("AR-2026-x1llnO1")
print(line)

[ ]:

# Cell 4 — non-CODE type → DAPX-Anchor
# Any non-CODE artifact type produces a DAPX-Anchor tag
# Replace AR-2026-XXXXXX with a known RESEARCH or DATA anchor AR-ID from Sepolia
line = watermark("AR-2026-XXXXXX", artifact_type="RESEARCH")
print(line)
# → DAPX-Anchor: anchorregistry.ai/AR-2026-XXXXXX

[ ]:

# Cell 5 — embed in a README or source file
readme_line = f"# My Project\n\n{watermark('AR-2026-x1llnO1')}"
print(readme_line)
